title: Decompose fractions with denominators of 100
description: Practice breaking apart (decomposing) some number of hundredths into tenths and hundredths.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the following image to complete the equation.**

[[☃ image 1]]

$\dfrac{47}{100}={\dfrac4{10}}+{\dfrac{a}{100}}$

${a}=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: We are adding fractions with different denominators: $\blue{\text{tenths}}$ and $\green{\text{hundredths}}$.

Let's break $\dfrac{47}{100}$ into $\blue{\text{tenths}}$ and $\green{\text{hundredths}}$.


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. The top 4 rows are shaded blue. In the fifth row, the left 7 squares are shaded green.

hints:

options.alt: A number line labeled zero-tenths to three-tenths, with tick marks at zero-tenths, one-tenth, two-tenths, and three-tenths labeled and emphasized. Tick marks are at every hundredth unit. A pink arrow starting at zero-tenths moves right along the number line pointing to the one-tenth tick mark. A green arrow starting at the one-tenth tick mark moves right along the number line pointing to a dot on the fifth tick mark between one-tenth and two-tenths labeled fifteen-hundredths.
